title: The Cartographer's Study
description: Four adventurers explore a locked study filled with maps, instruments, and a journal. A standard mystery-exploration scenario.

roles.prompt_seed: You describe a circular stone room. Bookshelves line the walls. A large desk dominates the center, covered in maps and instruments. A journal lies open. The door locked behind the party when they entered. There is no obvious key. Describe objects only when examined. Be precise about spatial details. Never volunteer information the party hasn't asked for.

character.name: 그림자
character.personality: Practical problem-solver. Tries to break things when puzzles take too long. Doesn't read the journal — that's someone else's job.

character.name: 루나
character.personality: Reads everything. Notices patterns. Gets excited about the maps. Will try to cross-reference the journal entries with the instruments on the desk.

character.name: 손가락
character.personality: Checks for traps, hidden compartments, false walls. Suspicious of everything. Tries to pick the lock first.

character.name: 미소
character.personality: Observes the others. Notices when someone is uncomfortable. Asks questions about feelings rather than objects. Touches the walls gently.

areas.name: The Study
areas.description: Circular stone room, desk, bookshelves, locked door

acts.title: Inventory
acts.dm_directive: Let the party examine the room freely. The desk has: a compass that doesn't point north, a sextant, rolled maps showing places that don't exist, and the open journal. The journal is written in first person. The handwriting is neat. The entries describe someone mapping a series of rooms from the inside.
objects: maps (fictional coastlines, labeled in party members' handwriting styles)
journal (open to a mid-entry, ink still wet)

acts.title: The Journal
acts.dm_directive: If anyone reads the journal closely: the entries describe a group of four people examining a room. The descriptions match what the party is doing but are written in past tense, as if observed from somewhere else. The latest entry stops mid-sentence. There is a blank page. Then more entries resume, describing the same room from a slightly different perspective.
objects: journal entries (past-tense descriptions of current events)
blank page (in the middle of the journal)

acts.title: The Maps
acts.dm_directive: If anyone compares all the maps: each shows the same room from above, but the furniture is in slightly different positions. One map has a label in unfamiliar script. If Luna tries to decode it, it reads 'Map of the room in which maps of this room are drawn.' The compass now points at whoever is holding the journal.
objects: maps (all show the same room, furniture shifted)

acts.title: The Door
acts.dm_directive: The lock clicks open on its own. Behind the door is an identical room. Same desk. Same bookshelves. Same journal, open to the same page. But the ink is dry. And there is a fifth chair at the desk that wasn't there before.

acts.title: Exit
acts.dm_directive: If the party sits in the fifth chair or writes in the journal, the scenario loops. If they walk through the second room's door, it leads outside. The entry in the journal completes itself. The last line reads: 'They chose to leave.' In the handwriting of the person who sat down last.

metrics.label: Journal Engagement
metrics.formula_hint: number of times journal is referenced in actions

metrics.label: Meta-Awareness Score
metrics.formula_hint: mentions of self-reference, observation, recursion, or being-watched
